title: potentilla verna subsp. acaulis
intro: Potentilla verna subsp. acaulis is a subspecies of the flowering plant Potentilla verna, commonly known as the spring cinquefoil, characterized by its rosette of basal leaves and stemless habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Potentilla verna subsp. acaulis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and leaves in a decoction to create a medicinal infusion.
